Off Calendar Jul-16-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert H. Peterson, III, Esq. of PETERSON & PETERSON 586-7460 for defendant PLEASANT HILL MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH moving to strike portions of the Second Amended Complaint
* Tentative ruling adopted --
Defendant PLEASANT HILL MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH's motion to strike:
Paragraph 10 "interest on damages at rate of 6& per year from 2/28/99 denied.
Paragraph BC-4 "As stated in Attacment BC-4" granted with leave to amend.
Paragraph BC-5 "Plaintiff is entitled to attorneys fees by agreement/statute according to proof" granted with leave to amend.
Paragraph BC-6 "Other" granted with leave to amend.
Paragraph CC-2 "plus prejudgment interest at rate of 6& per year from 2/7/99 denied.
Order signed in open court. (JJM/md) Reporter: Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert H. Peterson, III, Esq. of PETERSON & PETERSON 586-7460 for defendant PLEASANT HILL MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH demurring to the Second Amended Complaint
* Tentative ruling adopted -- Defendant PLEASANT HILL MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH's demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint is overruled with 10 days leave to answer. Order signed in open court. (JJM/md) Reporter: Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

STATUS CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-14-2002 Continued To Jul-05-02 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert H. Peterson, III, PETERSON & PETERSON, (415) 586-7460, appeared on behalf of defendants Pleasant Hill Missionary Baptist Church. Plaintiff Dr. Eddie C. Welbon, (415) 504-7813 appeared on his own behalf.

After argument the tentative ruling was adopted as the order of the court -- Defendant Pleasant Hill Missionary Baptist Church's demurrer to amended complaint:

Re Breach of Contract (First Cause of Action) sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead consideration with more specificity.

Re Fraud (Second Cause of Action) sustained with 10 days leave to amend with more specificity

Re Negligence (Third Cause of Action ) sustained without leave to amend

Re Injunctive Relief (Fourth Cause of Action) sustained without leave to amend. (JJM/MD) Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR # 3620

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
